project.creator: Codex
environment: 1. Frontend: React RequestDetailPage phase-1 workflow and RequestBrokerMarketPanel for client-side broker application review
2. Backend: NestJS POST /project-requests/:id/accept-broker and POST /project-requests/:id/release-broker-slot in ProjectRequestsController and ProjectRequestsService
3. Database: TypeORM project request and broker proposal relations with proposal status transitions and broker assignment persistence
4. Executed evidence: cmd /c npx jest src/modules/project-requests/project-requests.service.spec.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern="acceptBroker" passed on 25/03/2026 in server workspace
project.notes: Feature workbook for function 33 Application Viewing And Decisioning. The accept-broker service transitions are backed by a fresh targeted Jest run from this automation. Queue rendering, release-slot behavior, confirmation UX, and API error handling remain derived from the implemented React and NestJS flows and are currently marked Untested.

changeLog.description: Initial integration-style workbook covering client review of broker applications, broker selection, broker-slot release, and request phase advancement for the implemented request workflow.

features.name: Application Viewing And Decisioning
features.testRequirement: Validate client visibility over broker applications, hire-broker decisioning, proposal state transitions, broker-slot release controls, and phase-1 UI refresh behavior for the implemented request workflow.

functions.name: Broker Application Queue Viewing
functions.description: The client request detail workflow groups pending and non-pending broker proposals, shows cover letters and slot metrics, and exposes Hire Broker or Release Slot actions according to proposal state and viewer permissions.
functions.preCondition: The client opens a request detail page for a request still in a broker-recruitment state and the request payload includes brokerApplicationSummary and broker proposal items.

functions.name: Broker Selection And Rejection Decisioning
functions.description: The client confirms broker selection, the backend assigns the chosen broker, rejects competing pending proposals, optionally releases a broker slot, and the page refreshes the workflow state.
functions.preCondition: A request exists, the caller owns the request or holds an internal role for the release flow, and one or more broker proposals are available for action.

testCases.description: Render pending broker applications with cover letters and Hire Broker actions on a public draft request.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open the client request detail page for a PUBLIC_DRAFT request.
2. Switch to Workflow Management phase 1.
3. Inspect the Incoming Applications section in RequestBrokerMarketPanel.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. Pending broker applications are listed with broker name, status, applied date, and cover letter.
2. Each actionable candidate exposes a Hire Broker button.
3. Slot summary metrics and attachments are shown when present in the request payload.
testCases.dependencies: The request payload includes pending broker proposals and brokerApplicationSummary data.
testCases.note: Derived from RequestDetailPage currentPhase/tab setup and RequestBrokerMarketPanel pendingBrokerApplications rendering.

testCases.description: Hide the pending application list behind an empty state when no broker applications have been received.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open the client request detail page for a PUBLIC_DRAFT request with zero pending broker proposals.
2. Navigate to Workflow Management phase 1.
3. Inspect the Incoming Applications section.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The page shows the no-applications empty state.
2. No Hire Broker or Release Slot action is rendered for missing proposals.
3. The rest of the phase-1 discovery panel remains usable.
testCases.dependencies: The request payload contains zero pending broker proposals.
testCases.note: Derived from RequestBrokerMarketPanel conditional rendering for pendingBrokerApplications.length === 0.

testCases.description: Allow Release Slot only when the viewer permissions indicate broker-slot management is allowed.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open the client request detail page for a request with pending broker proposals.
2. Inspect a pending application card once with viewerPermissions.canReleaseBrokerSlot true and once with it false.
3. Compare the action area.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. Release Slot appears only when viewerPermissions.canReleaseBrokerSlot is true.
2. Hire Broker remains available for actionable candidates with a broker id.
3. The UI does not expose release controls when the permission is absent.
testCases.dependencies: The request payload includes pending broker proposals and toggles viewerPermissions.canReleaseBrokerSlot.
testCases.note: Derived from RequestBrokerMarketPanel action rendering rules.

testCases.description: Accept the selected broker, mark the request BROKER_ASSIGNED, and reject competing pending proposals.
testCases.procedure:
1. Run cmd /c npx jest src/modules/project-requests/project-requests.service.spec.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern="acceptBroker" in the server workspace.
2. Inspect the test case for the successful acceptBroker path.
3. Verify the saved request and proposal state transitions.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The Jest case passes.
2. requestRepo.save persists brokerId for the selected broker and status BROKER_ASSIGNED.
3. brokerProposalRepo.update marks the selected proposal ACCEPTED.
4. Other pending proposals for the same request are saved as REJECTED.
5. The service returns the refreshed request read-model.
testCases.actualResults: Targeted Jest execution passed. The service saved the request with brokerId broker-1 and status BROKER_ASSIGNED, updated the chosen proposal to ACCEPTED, rejected the competing pending proposal, and returned the refreshed request.
testCases.dependencies: server/node_modules is available and the project-requests service spec executes successfully.
testCases.note: Executed evidence from project-requests.service.spec.ts describe('acceptBroker') first test.

testCases.description: Reject broker acceptance when the request is no longer in a broker-selectable state.
testCases.procedure:
1. Run cmd /c npx jest src/modules/project-requests/project-requests.service.spec.ts --runInBand --testNamePattern="acceptBroker" in the server workspace.
2. Inspect the invalid-state acceptBroker case.
3. Observe the thrown error and repository side effects.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The Jest case passes.
2. acceptBroker throws the invalid-state error.
3. The request and proposal repositories are not mutated for the failed decision.
testCases.actualResults: Targeted Jest execution passed. The service threw 'Request is not in a valid state to accept a broker' for a COMPLETED request, and the request/proposal repositories were not updated.
testCases.dependencies: server/node_modules is available and the project-requests service spec executes successfully.
testCases.note: Executed evidence from project-requests.service.spec.ts describe('acceptBroker') second test.

testCases.description: Require a client confirmation step before the page sends the hire-broker API request.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open the client request detail page with at least one actionable broker application.
2. Click Hire Broker on a candidate card.
3. Confirm the warning modal flow before the API request is issued.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The first click stores the pending broker id and opens the confirmation warning.
2. No accept-broker API request is sent until the user confirms.
3. Confirming the modal triggers handleAcceptBroker for the stored broker id and clears the pending modal state.
testCases.dependencies: The request detail page loads a pending broker application with a broker id.
testCases.note: Derived from handleHireBrokerClick and handleConfirmHireBroker in RequestDetailPage.tsx.

testCases.description: Refresh phase-1 request data and show success feedback after a broker is hired from the client page.
testCases.procedure:
1. Open the client request detail page with a hireable broker application.
2. Complete the hire confirmation flow.
3. Observe the page after wizardService.acceptBroker resolves successfully.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. wizardService.acceptBroker posts brokerId to /project-requests/:id/accept-broker.
2. A success toast reports Broker Hired.
3. fetchData reloads the updated request.
4. The phase-1 panel switches from recruitment view to the Broker Hired success state and allows phase advancement.
testCases.dependencies: The accept-broker API call succeeds and returns an updated request payload.
testCases.note: Derived from handleAcceptBroker in RequestDetailPage.tsx and the currentPhase >= 2 success branch in RequestBrokerMarketPanel.tsx.

testCases.description: Release a broker slot by rejecting the active proposal when the actor is the owning client or internal staff before broker assignment.
testCases.procedure:
1. Call POST /project-requests/:id/release-broker-slot with a valid proposalId for a pending or invited broker proposal on an unassigned request.
2. Inspect ProjectRequestsService.releaseBrokerSlot behavior.
3. Reload the request detail after the call.
testCases.expectedResults:
1. The service verifies the actor is the owning client or an internal role.
2. The request must not already have brokerId assigned.
3. The targeted proposal status becomes REJECTED.
4. Notification payloads are created for the broker and client.
5. The refreshed request detail reflects the released slot.
testCases.dependencies: The caller is authorized, the request has no assigned broker, and the target broker proposal exists in an active application state.
